name: Citrus Caramel Flan
recipeIngredient: 2 strips of orange peel (3x1 inch), only the orange part
recipeInstructions:
1. Divide the orange peel among six 3/4-cup custard cups.
2. In a heavy small saucepan, combine sugar and lemon juice. Stir over low heat until sugar dissolves.
3. Increase heat to medium-high; boil without stirring until syrup turns golden (about 7 minutes). Brush down sides of the pan with a wet pastry brush to prevent sugar crystals from forming. Add water and swirl the pan occasionally.
4. Pour caramel into custard cups, dividing evenly over the orange peel strips.
5. Preheat oven to 325°F.
6. Combine milk, orange peel, cloves, and cinnamon in a heavy medium saucepan. Heat until tiny bubbles form around the edge of the pan (about 15 minutes).
7. Strain the mixture through a fine sieve into a bowl, discarding solids.
8. In a separate bowl, whisk eggs, egg yolk, sugar, and vanilla extract to blend.
9. Gradually whisk in warm milk while stirring constantly.
10. Pour the custard over the caramel in each cup.
11. Place the cups in a large baking pan and transfer to the oven.
12. Carefully add enough boiling water to the pan so that it comes halfway up the sides of the cups.
13. Bake until the custards are set and a knife inserted into the center comes out clean (about 1 hour 5 minutes).
14. Cool in the water bath on a rack for 2 hours.
15. Remove cups from the water bath; cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate overnight.
16. Loosen edges of caramel with the tip of a small knife, invert onto plates, letting caramel drip over the custard.
PER SERVING: calories, 180; fat, 4 g; sodium, 63 mg; cholesterol, 113 mg
